change_summary: EXP-282 integrated: defense neutralization paragraph (~1124 chars) inserted into detail_evidence between the 'consistent with a sun at 150 million km' anchor and the 'dome's-own-documentation' optical-illusion paragraph. Paragraph adds a projective-geometry argument that two-horizon convergence is the visual signature of parallel rays specifically, with a quantitative ~1 degree/100 km smear estimate for the finite-source alternative. No other WIN-026 fields modified.
our_argument_summary: WIN-026 claims crepuscular ray divergence confirms a local sun at ~5,733 km. We refute this on three independent grounds: (1) anticrepuscular rays converge at the anti-solar point — geometrically impossible for a finite source (now strengthened by EXP-282's projective-geometry framework); (2) the dome's own model documentation labels 5,733 km an 'optical illusion due to aetheric refraction,' so a WIN that depends on geometry-at-5,733-km is internally inconsistent; (3) monitor.py compares the predicted altitude against itself (hardcoded both sides), so the script never tests the WIN's empirical content. Verdict 'Refuted by Data' is appropriate and unchanged.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: monitor.py is not present in raw-text/ but per c1/c2 audits it hardcodes both predicted (5,733 km) and observed values, performing self-equality. No external API call. The WIN's empirical content (anti-solar convergence) is never tested.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Hardcoded on both sides (per c1/c2 audit). The dome's own 07-ai-context.txt line 24 ('The 5,733 km Sun altitude is an OPTICAL illusion') is a direct concession from the dome that the figure is not an empirical altitude.
data_sources_used: none — both sides hardcoded to dome-internal parameter
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Not applicable — there is no comparison logic to escape from. The script just compares a hardcoded value against itself.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None (post-c4 patch, the misapplied 'textbook physics' template was already replaced with the correct hardcoded-both-sides framing). EXP-282 did not touch detail_verdict_text, so c4's tag findings carry forward.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: c1/c2 audit shows monitor.py domain 34 hardcodes 5,733 on both sides. The dome's own 07-ai-context.txt line 24 ('The 5,733 km Sun altitude is an OPTICAL illusion') corroborates: no live measurement is being made — the value is a model-internal parameter. EXP-282 integration did not alter this; tag carries forward unchanged from c4.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard physics explains crepuscular ray divergence as perspective on parallel rays from an effectively-infinite source. The dome instead claims actual divergence from a local source at 5,733 km — a substantively different physical claim. The newly-inserted projective-geometry paragraph reinforces this: parallel-ray and finite-source geometries are not the same effect under a different name; they have different observational signatures (one vs two vanishing points).
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Both predicted and 'observed' values in monitor.py are hardcoded to the same dome-internal parameter. There is no independent measurement. Crepuscular ray photography long predates the dome model. Tag unchanged from c4.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: 5,733 km is an input parameter to the dome model, not derived within WIN-026 from dome geometry. The new EXP-282 paragraph also does not introduce any dome-derived numerical prediction; its math is generic projective geometry. Tag unchanged from c4.

holes_found.description: Asymmetric strength of dismissal in the inserted paragraph. The closing sentence — 'no aetheric-refraction caveat about the 5,733 km figure can manufacture parallel rays from a finite source' — is asserted, not argued. A determined dome defender will note that 'aetheric-refraction caveat about the 5,733 km figure' is a narrower target than the broader 'aetheric collimation by a refractive medium' move: a sufficiently-tuned gradient-index medium can in principle quasi-collimate rays from a finite point source (this is what a well-corrected refracting telescope does in reverse). The fuller counter — that any n(r) profile satisfying that constraint is over-determined when conjoined with apparent solar diameter, Mie/Rayleigh scattering, and overall sky-brightness — exists in EXP-282's anticipated_objections but is NOT in the published text. Net effect: defense_survives drops from 3 to ~1, but residual unfalsifiable-collimation hand-waving remains.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: add one sentence acknowledging the aetheric-collimation move and pointing out it requires an undisclosed n(r) profile that simultaneously preserves apparent solar diameter (~0.53 degrees) and standard scattering — at which point anti-solar convergence is being 'explained' by parameter fit, not predicted. Decider's call. Not blocking — the verdict is unchanged.

holes_found.description: Quantitative discriminator is presented in absolute terms ('1 degree per 100 km of baseline') without an observational-tolerance comparison. A naive reader could conclude that ~1 degree smear is barely discriminable from a vanishing point. In practice, anticrepuscular ray photographs (e.g., the Atmospheric Optics references already cited) typically show meeting points sharp to well under a degree, so for cloud-gap baselines on the order of 100 km the predicted smear is empirically excluded. The strength of the discriminator is real but the paragraph leaves the reader to infer it.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional micro-edit: append a clause noting that documented anticrepuscular-ray photographs show convergence sharp to well under one degree, ruling out a 5,733 km source for typical cloud-gap baselines. Decider's call. Argument is sound as-is; this would just tighten the rhetorical landing.

holes_found.description: Internal redundancy in detail_evidence has grown. There are now FIVE statements doing similar rhetorical work on the anti-solar point: (i) 'anticrepuscular rays converge at the anti-solar point simultaneously — a result impossible with a local sun'; (ii) 'A local sun at 5,733 km could not produce rays converging at both horizons simultaneously'; (iii) 'converging rays at the anti-solar point require the rays to remain straight over the full sky hemisphere, which is geometrically incompatible with any finite source'; (iv) 'Reconvergence at the anti-solar point is the geometric signature of a source at effectively infinite distance'; and now (v) the new paragraph's 'Two-horizon convergence is therefore a discriminator.' The c4 review flagged the first three as 'three-fold restatement.' The EXP-282 paragraph adds genuinely new content (projective-geometry framework + smear quantification), but the older restatements weren't trimmed when the new paragraph was inserted. This is editorial drag, not a defender-exploitable hole.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional consolidation: keep the new paragraph and one short pre-paragraph anti-solar statement; trim the other three restatements. Decider's call. The argument is fully sound; smoothness only.

holes_found.description: Stylistic — the is italicization on 'that is what a vanishing point is' reads as overemphasis. The surrounding prose is already pedagogical and the term is being introduced cleanly without the italics. Not a defender-exploitable issue; pure typography.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: drop the <em> tags. Decider's call.

stronger_arguments.description: Multi-station parallax argument (carried forward from c3 stronger_arguments and reinforced by EXP-282's geometry). The new paragraph's '~1 degree per 100 km of baseline at 5,733 km altitude' is exactly the same scaling that defeats a local sun via parallax: two observers separated by 100 km should see the sun in directions differing by ~1 degree. This is a unified geometric argument: the same divergence that prevents anti-solar convergence also produces multi-station parallax, and both are absent in observation. Worth flagging as a candidate for cross-WIN consolidation in some future expansion (links cleanly with WIN-005 sun-altitude triangulation).
sources: Standard projective geometry (e.g., Coxeter, Projective Geometry; Berger, Geometry I)

stronger_arguments.description: Photographic test from anti-solar geometry. Anticrepuscular ray meeting points in published photographs are typically sharp to well under 1 degree. A defender asserting aetheric collimation should be challenged to specify the n(r) profile that produces (a) sub-degree convergence at the anti-solar point AND (b) ~0.53 degree apparent solar disc AND (c) standard scattering — and then to derive (not retrofit) the apparent solar altitude as a consequence.

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome defender's 'perspective cuts both ways' move correctly identifies that crepuscular divergence at the sun and anticrepuscular convergence at the anti-solar point are not two independent optical phenomena but a single perspective effect viewed from two parts of the celestial sphere. That is a real geometric fact about projective geometry. The dome author has spotted a genuine symmetry.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The symmetry is precisely what makes two-horizon convergence a DISCRIMINATOR rather than ambiguous evidence. Both vanishing points exist together if and only if the underlying rays are mutually parallel — this is the projective-geometry definition of a vanishing point. A finite source emits non-parallel rays whose sky projections share the source point but never its antipode; the symmetric perspective the defender invokes does not apply because the rays are not parallel. The kernel ('perspective explains both ends') is the very fact that rules out a finite-altitude source. EXP-282's inserted paragraph now states this explicitly in published text.

advocate_mode.best_defense: Aetheric collimation by a non-trivial gradient-index medium converts radial rays from a finite source into quasi-parallel rays at the lower atmosphere. The inserted projective-geometry paragraph applies to RAW finite-source geometry, but the dome model never claimed raw finite-source geometry; it claims aetheric optics. The two-vanishing-point signature is therefore consistent with the dome too. The published text dismisses this in one sentence ('no aetheric-refraction caveat ... can manufacture parallel rays from a finite source'), but this is asserted, not argued.
advocate_mode.defense_survives_change: Down from 3 (c3, c4) to 1 (c5). The 'perspective cuts both ways' core defense is now closed — the inserted paragraph correctly identifies that two antipodal vanishing points are the projective signature of mutual parallelism. What remains is the unfalsifiable-collimation residue: a defender can always posit some unspecified aetheric mechanism. That is hand-waving rather than an active geometric argument, hence defense_survives=1.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Optional follow-up EXP if defense_survives=1 is judged worth driving to 0: add a paragraph specifying that any aetheric n(r) sufficient to collimate rays from 5,733 km also has to preserve apparent solar diameter and standard scattering; show this is over-determined, or equivalently that the n(r) is being parameter-fit rather than derived. Probably not worth the effort — defense_survives=1 is a healthy ceiling. Recommend closing this thread.

cross_win_consistency.notes: EXP-282 explicitly noted no cross-WIN cascade is required: WIN-005 and WIN-067/068 use the 5,733 km parameter for different geometric arguments and don't share the anti-solar-convergence reasoning. Verified — no parallel insertion needed. The new ~1 degree/100 km parallax scaling derived in WIN-026's paragraph is, however, the same scaling that defeats WIN-005's local-sun-altitude triangulation; this is consistency, not contradiction, and could be cross-referenced in some future consolidation pass.

checked: arctan(100/5733) = arctan(0.01744) = 0.999 degrees ~ 1.0 degree — claim '1 degree per 100 km of baseline at 5,733 km' is correct.
arctan(5/(5733-10)) = arctan(5/5723) = 0.0501 degrees — earlier 0.05 degree figure (already in detail_evidence pre-EXP-282) is correct.

These two scalings are consistent: angular spread = arctan(baseline / source_distance). The paragraph uses 'altitude' as the source distance, valid because altitude ~ source distance for rays viewed near the zenith.

Apparent solar diameter ~0.53 degree figure mentioned in stronger_arguments is the standard textbook value for the Sun's angular diameter (it is also implicit in EXP-282's anticipated_objections #1). Verified.

citations_verified: atoptics.co.uk/blog/anticrepuscular-rays/ — cited in detail_evidence; existing reference, not modified by EXP-282.
atoptics.co.uk/blog/crepuscular-rays/ — cited in detail_evidence; existing reference, not modified by EXP-282.
raw-text/05-model.txt '(optical illusion due to aetheric refraction)' — cited in detail_evidence; verified verbatim against raw-text/05-model.txt in c4.
citations_unchecked: EXP-282's anticipated_objections cite Coxeter (Projective Geometry) and Berger (Geometry I) as sources for the great-circle-arc/two-vanishing-point claim. These are textbook citations and not in the published WIN-026 text — only in EXP-282's reasoning trail. No published-text citation issue.

deeper_analysis: EXP-282's mandate was to close the c3 advocate-identified hole (defense_survives=3): the dome defender's strongest move was to claim anti-solar convergence is itself a perspective effect, symmetric to the perspective explanation we accept for crepuscular divergence at the sun direction. If accepted, the symmetry would erode our anti-solar argument as a discriminator. The inserted paragraph closes this loophole by stating the projective-geometry fact: two antipodal vanishing points are the signature of mutual parallelism, and finite sources don't produce mutual parallelism. The argument is correct, the math (~1 degree/100 km) is correct, the cross-WIN cascade analysis is correct, and the integration is clean (single insertion at the documented anchor, no other fields touched, length delta matches: 1789 -> 2913 = +1124 chars, matches EXP-282's replacement_word_count.delta_chars). The remaining defender moves are (a) unfalsifiable aetheric-collimation hand-waving, addressed in EXP-282's reasoning trail but only one-line dismissed in the published text, and (b) editorial drag from accumulating restatements — neither is a verdict-relevant hole. Confidence stays at 0.94 (effectively unchanged from c4's 0.95; the moderate hole closes but minor editorial drag has accumulated). Recommended action: no_change. ISS-1790 should be marked closed; ISS-1791 and ISS-1792 (still open from c4) remain unchanged by this integration.
summary_for_decider: EXP-282 successfully closes ISS-1790. The inserted paragraph correctly answers the c3/c4 advocate move using a projective-geometry argument (two antipodal vanishing points are the signature of mutual parallelism) plus a quantitative ~1 degree/100 km smear estimate. defense_survives drops from 3 to 1. Remaining hole is unfalsifiable aetheric-collimation hand-waving, which is one-line dismissed in the published text but fully refuted only in EXP-282's anticipated_objections (not published). Four minor findings logged, all optional — none block. ISS-1790 should be marked closed; ISS-1791 and ISS-1792 carry forward unchanged. Recommend no further verdict change.
